data.title: Tonner Drones allocates exit funds into SpaceX, focusing on aerospace and satellite infrastructure growth ($ALTD).
data.description: Tonner Drones, with the stock ticker symbol ALTD, has decided to reinvest the proceeds from exiting Donecle into a strategic move by investing in SpaceX, a prominent company in the aerospace sector. This strategic investment aims to tap into the high-growth potential of aerospace and satellite infrastructure. The exact amount of the investment has not been disclosed publicly. This decision highlights Tonner Drones' focus on making strategic investments to further its growth and development within the industry.

data.title: Thatcher & Company appoints Todd Irwin as Managing Director and COO. Bringing technology expertise and scaling experience. Jan 28, 2026.
data.description: Experienced communications specialist Todd Irwin has been appointed as Managing Director of Thatcher & Company in New York. With deep technology knowledge and successful agency scaling background, Irwin will play a key role in driving the firm's growth in the coming period. The announcement was made on January 28, 2026.

data.title: ISSGlobal launches ISS365, offering 24/7 security monitoring to protect corps from cyber threats - eliminating the need for internal operations.
data.description: ISSGlobal has introduced ISS365, a service designed to safeguard businesses from cyber threats. The solution aims to provide round-the-clock security without the need for companies to create their own internal security operations. Based in Miami, the global cyber-consultancy firm offers managed security and compliance services. The launch was announced on January 28, 2026.

data.title: ACEC California and Scholarship Foundation to honor 20 engineering projects in partnership with public agencies and private companies.
data.description: In Sacramento, California on January 28, 2026, the American Council of Engineering Companies, California (ACEC California) along with the ACEC California Scholarship Foundation, will be honoring twenty engineering projects. This event is being organized in collaboration with various public sector agencies and private companies.

data.title: Synoptek wins Five9 Global Partner Award for 2025 & Canada Partner of the Year for its Teams+ platform.
data.description: Synoptek, based in Costa Mesa, California, was recently honored for their innovative Teams+ platform for unified voice integration. The company proudly disclosed that they were awarded the prestigious 2025 Five9 Global Partner Award. Furthermore, Synoptek was specifically named the Canada Partner of the Year by Five9. The recognition showcases the company's dedication to excellence in the industry and highlights their commitment to providing top-tier services.

data.title: $7 million donation from MacArthur Foundation & Whittier Trust spurs matching challenge for Robert K. Ross Center in LA.
data.description: On January 28, 2026, Linc Housing Corporation revealed a significant injection of catalytic capital to propel forward the Robert K. Ross Center for Hope and Healing and associated community development projects in downtown Los Angeles. This initiative aims to revitalize the area and improve livability. The funding, amounting to [specific amount], will support the construction of affordable housing units and provide essential services to [number] individuals in need. This boost in financial support underscores a commitment to fostering growth and well-being within the community.

data.title: Empathy partners with Transamerica for Loss Support and LifeVault to help 10 million customers.
data.description: In New York today, Empathy revealed a significant partnership with Transamerica, a major player in life insurance and financial security, catering to over 10 million clients and overseeing assets worth $400 billion. This collaboration introduces Empathy Loss Support and Empathy LifeVault, innovative services aimed at aiding individuals in dealing with post-loss challenges and organizing their digital plans.

Members of Israeli Knesset sent a letter to former U.S. President Joe Biden in which they expressed regret over the "shameful and false statements" of Prime Minister Netanyahu, who yesterday blamed Biden for the deaths of Israeli soldiers in Gaza.

data.title: Southwest transitions after 50 years, now assigns seats. New system began on Tuesday; passengers' preference considered.
data.description: The airline recently introduced assigned seating on Tuesday, which caters to passengers' preferences. However, despite the change, some customers believe that the previous scramble for seats added a unique charm to the experience.

data.title: Apollo Silver Corp. closes $27.5 million private placement, with $12.5 million investment from Jupiter Asset Management; total of 2.5 million units issued at $5.00 per unit.
data.description: Apollo Silver Corp. has successfully completed the final tranche of its non-brokered private placement, as of January 28, 2026. In this tranche, the company issued 2,500,000 units at a price of $5.00 per unit, resulting in total gross proceeds of $12,500,000. This marks the closure of the upsized Offering announced on January 21, 2026. The entire Offering has been fully subscribed, raising a total of $27,500,000 in gross proceeds. Apollo Silver Corp. operates under the tickers TSX.V:APGO, OTCQB:APGOF, and Frankfurt:6ZF0.

data.title: South Dakota reintroduces Bitcoin reserve bill to invest up to 10% of public funds in BTC, emphasizing strict security measures.
data.description: South Dakota Republican Representative Logan Manhart has introduced House Bill 1155, which proposes allowing the state to invest up to 10% of public funds in Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC). This comes a year after a similar bill was rejected due to concerns about its volatility. The bill grants South Dakota's State Investment Council the authority to invest 10% of eligible state funds in Bitcoin through three options: direct holding with state control, using qualified custodians like regulated banks, or purchasing regulated Bitcoin ETFs. The legislation enforces strict security protocols, requiring the state to manage its private keys, store them in encrypted hardware across secure locations, mandate multiple approvals for transactions, and conduct regular security audits. House Bill 1155 will now move to the Committee on Commerce and Energy for further review.
